<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Perfect isolation (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0) confirms the e2e suite treats the CLI as a true black box — a strong architectural signal that the public CLI contract is stable and testable without internal access.
[observation] [info] cli-recommend.test.ts and cli-smart-add.test.ts target the two most complex commands; ensuring these tests cover failure paths and edge inputs will protect the highest-risk user-facing flows.
[relationship] [info] The stark contrast between e2e-tests (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0) and unit-cli (cohesion 0.34) suggests the project's test strategy is bifurcated: end-to-end tests are disciplined and isolated while unit tests have accumulated without a consistent home; a zone-aligned unit test convention (co-locate test files with the zone they cover) would close this gap
[suggestion] [warning] Add a dependency-cruiser or eslint-plugin-import rule that forbids e2e test files from importing any path outside tests/e2e/ or node built-ins. The current perfect isolation (coupling 0) is convention-only and will silently degrade as the test suite grows.
[suggestion] [warning] Add e2e tests for the 'health' and 'fix' CLI commands. These commands have production files in prd-tree-operations and prd-store-sync respectively but no corresponding e2e coverage visible in the zone, leaving two non-trivial user-facing flows unprotected at the integration boundary.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling confirm a fully isolated test layer — no production code bleeds in, which is ideal
- Coverage spans most major CLI commands (adapter, analyze, import, prune, recommend, smart-add, sync) — a healthy breadth signal for regression confidence
- Zero coupling means these tests invoke the CLI as a black box; if internal behavior needs direct assertion, a separate integration layer already exists in tests/integration/
- Perfect isolation (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0) confirms the e2e suite treats the CLI as a true black box — a strong architectural signal that the public CLI contract is stable and testable without internal access.
- cli-recommend.test.ts and cli-smart-add.test.ts target the two most complex commands; ensuring these tests cover failure paths and edge inputs will protect the highest-risk user-facing flows.
- The stark contrast between e2e-tests (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0) and unit-cli (cohesion 0.34) suggests the project's test strategy is bifurcated: end-to-end tests are disciplined and isolated while unit tests have accumulated without a consistent home; a zone-aligned unit test convention (co-locate test files with the zone they cover) would close this gap
- The e2e zone's perfect cohesion (1.0) is structurally fragile: a single production import added to any test file breaks the isolation property with no automated guard to catch it. The invariant exists by convention only.
- The e2e suite covers adapter, analyze, import, prune, quiet, recommend, smart-add, and sync commands but the file listing does not show coverage for the 'health' command (cli/commands/health.ts) or the 'fix' command (prd-store-sync) — both non-trivial commands with no apparent e2e test.
- Add a dependency-cruiser or eslint-plugin-import rule that forbids e2e test files from importing any path outside tests/e2e/ or node built-ins. The current perfect isolation (coupling 0) is convention-only and will silently degrade as the test suite grows.
- Add e2e tests for the 'health' and 'fix' CLI commands. These commands have production files in prd-tree-operations and prd-store-sync respectively but no corresponding e2e coverage visible in the zone, leaving two non-trivial user-facing flows unprotected at the integration boundary.
- [call graph] 238 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
